summary: AllianceChicago provides comments on the HTI-5 proposed rule focusing on deregulation of health IT certification requirements. The comment addresses the potential impacts of removing or reducing standards such as C-CDA creation, audit logs, and EHI export.
key_arguments: Reducing certification requirements may lower developer burden
Certain standards remain important for interoperability and patient safety
Balance needed between deregulation and maintaining data quality
Potential risks if audit logs and data export capabilities are weakened
recommendations: Retain essential interoperability standards like C-CDA creation
Maintain robust audit log requirements to ensure security
Ensure EHI export capabilities are preserved for data access
Implement burden reduction without compromising patient safety
notable_quote: AllianceChicago offers comments on HTI-5 to balance deregulation with interoperability and safety.
